metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=full driver=gap:wait_turn â€” agy auto-executes one run_command/list_dir mid-turn; parser emits assistant_message+ToolUse then function_call_output, never waiting; wait_turn is a driver stub.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## Reasoning

The scenario asserts a `ready â†’ working â†’ ready` arc with at least one tool_use mid-turn, and the invariant that state NEVER enters `waiting` for an auto-executed tool call.

### agent_supports: yes

Every real sample conversation under `~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/brain/` exercises exactly this: a `MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE` states intent ("I will list the contents of the workspace directory.") and carries a `tool_calls` array (`list_dir` or `run_command`), and the following `MODEL/<TYPE>` line (`LIST_DIRECTORY` / `RUN_COMMAND`) is the auto-executed RESULT. The `agy` CLI auto-runs its tools by default (and `--dangerously-skip-permissions` makes that unconditional); the samples were recorded with tools auto-approving, no user gate. The toolset (`run_command`, `list_dir`, `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `view_file`, `grep_search`) is confirmed in the binary strings.

### daemon_capability: full

Walking the signal against `parser.go`:

1. **`ready â†’ working`** â€” `parseUserInput` emits `user_message` (ClearToolNames) on the opening `USER_INPUT`.
2. **tool_use mid-turn** â€” `parsePlannerResponse` on a planner step WITH `tool_calls` emits `EventType = "assistant_message"` plus a synthesized `ToolUse` (id `<step_index>-<i>` since antigravity carries no tool ids; parser.go ~L120-137). It harvests the cwd from a `run_command`'s `Cwd` arg onto every event for PID binding.
3. **tool result, no waiting** â€” the next `MODEL/<TYPE>` result line hits `parseToolResult` â†’ `EventType = "function_call_output"`, which CLOSES the open tool (ToolResultIDs) and is explicitly a NON-settling event so the turn stays `working` until the terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE (parser.go ~L143-152, docstring confirms "the turn stays working until the terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE"). The antigravity adapter declares NO permission-gate event path on a tool call â€” the parser maps tool execution to assistant_message/function_call_output only, never a `waiting` state. So the `state never entered waiting` invariant holds structurally.
4. **`working â†’ ready`** â€” the terminal empty `MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE` â†’ `turn_done` â†’ `ready`.

Topology `ready â†’ working â†’ ready` with â‰Ą1 tool call and no `waiting` is exactly what the parser produces today â†’ `daemon=full`.

### driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

Same driver scaffold limit as the other lifecycle cells: the recipe needs `send` + `wait_turn` + `sleep`, and `wait_turn()` is a stub (`not_implemented`; DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"). The auto-executed tool turn ends on a terminal empty PLANNER_RESPONSE; wait_turn must poll for that. `record` ports the wait_turn seam + the launch_repl session-resolution SEAM before driving. INTERACTIVE recipe (headless --print exits before the daemon sees the terminal workingâ†’ready edge).
caveats: Tokens/cost are NOT in the antigravity JSONL (per-conversation SQLite protobuf store the parser does not read); observations assert tokens_nonzero:false, cost_nonzero:false. Not a daemon bug â€” the lifecycle arc + tool_use are fully observed.

No-waiting invariant is structural: the antigravity parser maps a tool call to assistant_message/function_call_output and never to a waiting state (the adapter declares no permission-gate path on tool execution), so under auto-approve the session cannot flicker to waiting.
Provisional on emission: grounded in the parser read + four real samples (every one auto-executes list_dir/run_command). record promotes provisionalâ†’settled.
driver=gap:wait_turn: wait_turn is a driver stub; record ports it (and the launch_repl session-id/transcript SEAM) before driving.

sources.note: parsePlannerResponse WITH tool_calls â†’ assistant_message + synthesized ToolUse (id step_index-i, no native ids; ~L120-137); parseToolResult â†’ function_call_output, a NON-settling event closing the open tool so the turn stays working (~L143-152); no waiting-state path on tool execution.

Real sample: PLANNER_RESPONSE(tool_calls:[list_dir]) â†’ LIST_DIRECTORY result â†’ PLANNER_RESPONSE(tool_calls:[run_command]) â†’ RUN_COMMAND result â†’ terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE â€” auto-executed tools, no user gate.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; wait_turn() is a stub (not_implemented) â†’ driver=gap:wait_turn.

script.text: Run the shell command `echo hello` using your run_command tool, then reply with exactly the word: done
